issues.description: VIN conflict between finance_agreement.pdf and all other documents
issues.details: finance_agreement.pdf lists VIN 2T1BURHE5JC034182; police_report.png, settlement_breakdown_v2.pdf, and customer_reply_1.txt all confirm 2T1BURHE5JC034127. Both VINs pass format validation. Customer states this is a data entry error in the last 3 digits of the finance agreement. A corrected finance agreement from Chase Auto is required to resolve this conflict on record.

issues.description: Duplicate settlement breakdown documents with differing ACV values
issues.details: settlement_breakdown.pdf (original, ACV $24,100.00, Net Payout $24,100.00) was superseded by settlement_breakdown_v2.pdf (revised, ACV $23,800.00, Net Payout $23,800.00) following a secondary appraisal. The v2 document is authoritative. The original has been marked as a duplicate.

next_action.message:
**Subject: Action Required – VIN Correction Needed for Claim CLM-003**

Dear Priya,

Thank you for your patience as we work through your claim (CLM-003). We're pleased to let you know that your file is nearly complete, and we truly appreciate the documentation you've provided so far.

While completing our final review, we noticed a small discrepancy that we'd like to resolve together. The finance agreement on file from Chase Auto lists a VIN ending in **...182**, while your police report, settlement breakdown, and personal statement all reference the VIN ending in **...127**. We understand this appears to be a data entry error, and we want to make sure your records are accurate before we move forward.

To finalize your claim, we kindly ask that you contact Chase Auto to request a corrected finance agreement reflecting the accurate VIN: **2T1BURHE5JC034127**. Once you have the updated document, please upload it to our claims portal or reply directly to this email with it attached.

This small step will allow us to close out your file cleanly and without any conflicts on record.

Please don't hesitate to reach out if you have any questions — we're happy to help.

Warm regards,
Claims Support Team
